title: A line that reaches back through millennia.
paragraphs_before: Yoga was born in India, passed mouth to ear by the rishis and munis — seers who withdrew into forests and mountains and listened to the silence to understand the living. The practice is older than writing itself.

shloka.translation: Yoga is the stilling of the fluctuations of the mind.

paragraphs_after: What Jitendra transmits today in Paris — the breath, the posture, the silence — belongs to this unbroken line. Nothing is invented. Everything is remembered.
Ashtanga — Patañjali's eight limbs: yama, niyama, āsana, prāṇāyāma, pratyāhāra, dhāraṇā, dhyāna, samādhi — remains the map.
